title: agrostis pilosa var. mutica
intro: Agrostis pilosa var. mutica, also known as creeping bent or short red bent, is a type of perennial grass native to Asia. It is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant has been found to have health benefits such as reducing fever and relieving pain, as well as having antimicrobial properties that can help prevent infections. Agrostis pilosa var. mutica is traditionally prepared as a herbal tea or infusion for its medicinal uses, and it may also be used in combination with other herbs in traditional remedies.
